hook.text: Chalk cliffs are made from trillions of microscopic shells. Living creatures built rock without even trying.

buildup.text: Biochemical rocks form from the accumulated remains of organisms: shells, corals, plankton, and plant matter.

discovery.text: Most limestone is biochemical, built from coral reefs and shell fragments cemented on ancient sea floors.

twist.text: Coal is biochemical too. It formed from compressed swamp plants over 300 million years of burial and heat.

climax.text: Life itself creates rock. Biochemical deposits prove that biology and geology are deeply intertwined.

punchline.text: Tiny creatures can build mountains of stone.

quiz.question: What is most limestone made from?
options: Accumulated shells, corals, and marine organisms
